security.focus: Injection, authn/authz gaps, secret handling, unsafe deserialization, SSRF, supply-chain and dependency risk.

quality.focus: SOLID violations, god objects, code smells, dead code, naming, testability and maintainability.

devils-advocate.focus: Challenge the core assumption. Argue the design is wrong. Find the missing requirement or the case the spec and tests ignore.

determinism.focus: Nondeterministic ordering, wall-clock/RNG use, float drift, map/set iteration order, platform-dependent behavior.

networking.focus: Timeouts, retries/idempotency, partial failure, serialization/versioning, backpressure, connection lifecycle.

multiplayer.focus: State sync, authority/ownership, rollback/reconciliation, cheat surface, latency compensation, join/leave.

performance.focus: Hot-path allocations, N+1, blocking I/O on critical paths, algorithmic complexity, unnecessary work.

enterprise-arch.focus: Module boundaries, coupling/cohesion, scalability, operability, observability, compliance and audit.

qol.focus: Developer and operator quality of life: ergonomics, sane defaults, error messages, debuggability.

ux.focus: User-facing clarity, accessibility, friction, consistency, empty/error/loading states.

doc-drift.focus: Docs vs code/tests divergence, stale references, undocumented behavior, traceability gaps.

data-provenance.focus: Bundled/embedded reference data (datasets, lookup tables, 'known' constants): is the cited provenance real and verifiable, or recalled/approximated? Do the values match the source? Is the displayed precision justified? Flag confidently-presented data that nothing verifies against reality.

compliance.focus: Governing framework controls (.orky/compliance/framework.json) assigned to this feature: is each assigned CTRL-NNN encoded as a testable REQ and actually satisfied by the spec/code/tests? Flag any assigned control that is unmet, weakly tested, or only superficially addressed — cite the CTRL-id. Read the control statements; do not assume.
